highlights.text: Today, I was looking for that momentum to continue a little higher, stating yesterday: “My general lean if I is that ES can try to recover the big 5988, 5996, 6004 resistance shelf.
As I’ve discussed at length, what I call ‘elevator down’ sells in ES begin when it loses a significant, previously heavily defended support shelf.
Just like how sells in ES are caused by major support shelves failing, bounces/squeezes are caused when ES puts in my core setup: The Failed Breakdown (bear trap).

I like this level better particularly if we can put in a Failed Breakdown of the February 2nd multi-week low which at 5935 now.” Yesterday morning, we sold down to 5934, put in a big Failed Breakdown of the February 2nd multi-week low, and we were off to the races to 5996 by yesterdays close.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the setup that caused yesterday mornings hard sell (my third setup type, the Breakdown Short) and yesterday/into todays violent squeeze (my core setup, the Failed Breakdown).

post_title: Can Nvidia Earnings Break The "Sell Bounces" Streak For SPX? Feb 27 Plan
